𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Ten — Part Two

The Book of Yehoshua

YEHOSHUA

The Conquest of the Land

Chapters 9 through 15

The Southern and Northern Campaigns — The Allotments Begin

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJosha (4Q47) — Cave 4 — Primary DSS witness for Yehoshua

4QJoshb (4Q48) — Cave 4 — Supporting DSS fragments

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon — Yehoshua preserved in full

YHWH and The Creator only — no substitute titles

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTERS 9 THROUGH 15 — CAMPAIGNS AND ALLOTMENTS

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 9 The Gibeonite Deception — The Covenant of Peace — Woodcutters and Water Carriers Forever

1 When all the kings who were beyond the Yarden, in the hill country and in the lowland and all the coast of the Great Sea toward Lebanon, the Khittites, the Amorites, the Kena'anites, the Perizzites, the Khivvites, and the Yevusites, heard of this,

2 They gathered together with one accord to fight against Yehoshua and Yisra'EL.

3 But when the inhabitants of Giv'on heard what Yehoshua had done to Yerikho and to Ai,

4 They on their part acted with cunning and went and made ready provisions and took worn-out sacks for their donkeys, and wineskins, worn-out and torn and mended,

5 With worn-out, patched sandals on their feet, and worn-out clothes. And all their provisions were dry and crumbly.

6 And they went to Yehoshua in the camp at Gilgal and said to him and to the men of Yisra'EL, We have come from a distant country, so now make a covenant with us.

7 But the men of Yisra'EL said to the Khivvites, Perhaps you live among us — then how can we make a covenant with you?

8 They said to Yehoshua, We are your servants. And Yehoshua said to them, Who are you? And where do you come from?

9 They said to him, From a very distant country your servants have come, because of the name of YHWH your Creator. For we have heard a report of him, and all that he did in Egypt,

10 And all that he did to the two kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Yarden, Sikhon the king of Kheshbon, and Og king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtarot.

11 So our elders and all the inhabitants of our country said to us, Take provisions in your hand for the journey and go to meet them and say to them, We are your servants. Come now, make a covenant with us.

12 Here is our bread. It was still warm when we took it from our houses as our food for the journey on the day we set out to come to you, but now, behold, it is dry and crumbly.

13 These wineskins were new when we filled them, and behold, they have burst. And these garments and sandals of ours are worn out from the very long journey.

14 So the men took some of their provisions, but did not ask counsel from the mouth of YHWH.

15 And Yehoshua made peace with them and made a covenant with them, to let them live, and the leaders of the congregation swore to them.

16 At the end of three days after they had made a covenant with them, they heard that they were their neighbors and that they lived among them.

17 And the people of Yisra'EL set out and reached their cities on the third day. Now their cities were Giv'on, Kefira, Be'erot, and Kiryat-Ye'arim.

18 But the people of Yisra'EL did not attack them, because the leaders of the congregation had sworn to them by YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL. Then all the congregation murmured against the leaders.

19 But all the leaders said to all the congregation, We have sworn to them by YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, and now we may not touch them.

20 This we will do to them: let them live, lest wrath come upon us, because of the oath that we swore to them.

21 And the leaders said to them, Let them live. So they became cutters of wood and drawers of water for all the congregation, just as the leaders had said of them.

22 Yehoshua summoned them, and he said to them, Why did you deceive us, saying, We are very far from you, when you dwell among us?

23 Now therefore you are cursed, and some of you shall never be anything but servants, cutters of wood and drawers of water for the house of my Creator.

24 They answered Yehoshua, Because it was told to your servants for a certainty that YHWH your Creator had commanded his servant Moshe to give you all the land and to destroy all the inhabitants of the land from before you — so we feared greatly for our lives because of you and did this thing.

25 And now, behold, we are in your hand. Whatever seems good and right in your sight to do to us, do it.

26 So he did this to them and delivered them out of the hand of the people of Yisra'EL, and they did not kill them.

27 But Yehoshua made them that day cutters of wood and drawers of water for the congregation and for the altar of YHWH, to this day, in the place that he should choose.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Yehoshua. The Giv'onite deception narrative is one of the most theologically instructive passages in Yehoshua — verse 14 identifies the critical failure: the men took of their provisions but did not ask counsel from the mouth of YHWH. The deception succeeded precisely because covenant inquiry was omitted.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. The binding power of the oath sworn to the Giv'onites — even obtained through deception — is read as the covenant principle that YHWH's name invoked in an oath creates an obligation that cannot be annulled by subsequent discovery of fraud.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 9. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The Giv'onites are identified as Khivvites in verse 7, one of the seven nations of Kena'an listed in Devarim 7:1.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — the men took some of their provisions but did not ask counsel from the mouth of YHWH. The entire chapter turns on this single failure. The deception was elaborate and effective — worn sandals, stale bread, burst wineskins. But the detection was straightforward: ask YHWH. The covenant failure is not that Yehoshua was deceived — it is that he did not consult the one who cannot be deceived. The oath sworn by YHWH's name then binds the covenant community even to the deceiver — verse 19-20. The covenant oath is more powerful than the covenant strategy that preceded it.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 10 The Sun Stands Still — Five Kings Defeated — YHWH Fought for Yisra'EL

1 As soon as Adoni-Tzedek king of Yerushalayim heard how Yehoshua had captured Ai and had devoted it to destruction, doing to Ai and its king as he had done to Yerikho and its king, and how the inhabitants of Giv'on had made peace with Yisra'EL and were among them,

2 He feared greatly, because Giv'on was a great city, like one of the royal cities, and because it was greater than Ai, and all its men were warriors.

3 So Adoni-Tzedek king of Yerushalayim sent to Hoham king of Khevron, to Piram king of Yarmut, to Yafiya king of Lakhish, and to Devir king of Eglon, saying,

4 Come up to me and help me, and let us strike Giv'on. For it has made peace with Yehoshua and with the people of Yisra'EL.

5 Then the five kings of the Amorites, the king of Yerushalayim, the king of Khevron, the king of Yarmut, the king of Lakhish, and the king of Eglon, gathered their forces and went up with all their armies and encamped against Giv'on and made war against it.

6 And the men of Giv'on sent to Yehoshua at the camp in Gilgal, saying, Do not relax your hand from your servants. Come up to us quickly and save us and help us, for all the kings of the Amorites who dwell in the hill country are gathered against us.

7 So Yehoshua went up from Gilgal, he and all the people of war with him, and all the mighty men of valor.

8 And YHWH said to Yehoshua, Do not fear them, for I have given them into your hands. Not a man of them shall stand before you.

9 So Yehoshua came upon them suddenly, having marched up all night from Gilgal.

10 And YHWH threw them into a panic before Yisra'EL, who struck them with a great blow at Giv'on and chased them by the way of the ascent of Beit-Kharon and struck them as far as Azekah and Maqkedah.

11 And as they fled before Yisra'EL, while they were going down the ascent of Beit-Kharon, YHWH threw down large stones from heaven on them as far as Azekah, and they died. There were more who died because of the hailstones than the sons of Yisra'EL killed with the sword.

12 At that time Yehoshua spoke to YHWH in the day when YHWH gave the Amorites over to the sons of Yisra'EL, and he said in the sight of Yisra'EL, Sun, stand still at Giv'on, and moon, in the Valley of Ayalon.

13 And the sun stood still, and the moon stopped, until the nation took vengeance on their enemies. Is this not written in the Book of the Upright? The sun stopped in the midst of heaven and did not hurry to set for about a whole day.

14 There has been no day like it before or since, when YHWH heeded the voice of a man, for YHWH fought for Yisra'EL.

15 So Yehoshua returned, and all Yisra'EL with him, to the camp at Gilgal.

16 These five kings fled and hid themselves in the cave at Maqkedah.

17 And it was told to Yehoshua, The five kings have been found, hidden in the cave at Maqkedah.

18 And Yehoshua said, Roll large stones against the mouth of the cave and set men by it to guard them,

19 But do not stay there yourselves. Pursue your enemies — attack their rear guard. Do not let them enter their cities, for YHWH your Creator has given them into your hand.

20 When Yehoshua and the sons of Yisra'EL had finished striking them with a great blow until they were wiped out, and when the remnant that remained of them had entered into the fortified cities,

21 All the people returned safe to Yehoshua in the camp at Maqkedah. Not a man moved his tongue against any of the people of Yisra'EL.

22 Then Yehoshua said, Open the mouth of the cave and bring those five kings out to me from the cave.

23 And they did so, and brought those five kings out to him from the cave, the king of Yerushalayim, the king of Khevron, the king of Yarmut, the king of Lakhish, and the king of Eglon.

24 And when they brought those kings out to Yehoshua, Yehoshua summoned all the men of Yisra'EL and said to the chiefs of the men of war who had gone with him, Come near and put your feet on the necks of these kings. And they came near and put their feet on their necks.

25 And Yehoshua said to them, Do not be afraid or dismayed — be strong and courageous. For thus YHWH will do to all your enemies against whom you fight.

26 And afterward Yehoshua struck them and put them to death, and he hanged them on five trees. And they hung on the trees until evening.

27 But at the time of the going down of the sun, Yehoshua commanded, and they took them down from the trees and threw them into the cave where they had hidden themselves, and they set large stones against the mouth of the cave, which remain to this very day.

28 As for Maqkedah, Yehoshua captured it on that day and struck it, and its king, with the edge of the sword. He devoted to destruction every person in it — he left none remaining. And he did to the king of Maqkedah just as he had done to the king of Yerikho.

29 Then Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL with him passed on from Maqkedah to Livnah and fought against Livnah.

30 And YHWH gave it also and its king into the hand of Yisra'EL. And he struck it with the edge of the sword, and every person in it — he left none remaining in it. And he did to its king as he had done to the king of Yerikho.

31 Then Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL with him passed on from Livnah to Lakhish and laid siege to it and fought against it.

32 And YHWH gave Lakhish into the hand of Yisra'EL, and he captured it on the second day and struck it with the edge of the sword, and every person in it, as he had done to Livnah.

33 Then Horam king of Gezer came up to help Lakhish. And Yehoshua struck him and his people, until he left none remaining.

34 Then Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL with him passed on from Lakhish to Eglon. And they laid siege to it and fought against it.

35 And they captured it on that day, and struck it with the edge of the sword. And every person in it he devoted to destruction that day, as he had done to Lakhish.

36 Then Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL with him went up from Eglon to Khevron. And they fought against it

37 And captured it and struck it with the edge of the sword, and its king and its towns, and every person in it. He left none remaining, as he had done to Eglon, and devoted it to destruction and every person in it.

38 Then Yehoshua and all Yisra'EL with him turned back to Devir and fought against it

39 And he captured it with its king and all its towns. And they struck them with the edge of the sword and devoted to destruction every person in it — he left none remaining. Just as he had done to Khevron and to Livnah and its king, so he did to Devir and to its king.

40 So Yehoshua struck the whole land, the hill country and the Negev and the lowland and the slopes, and all their kings. He left none remaining, but devoted to destruction all that breathed, just as YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL commanded.

41 And Yehoshua struck them from Kadesh-Barnea as far as Azzah, and all the country of Goshen, as far as Giv'on.

42 And Yehoshua captured all these kings and their land at one time, because YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL fought for Yisra'EL.

43 Then Yehoshua returned, and all Yisra'EL with him, to the camp at Gilgal.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 10. The account of the sun standing still — verses 12-14 — is one of the most discussed passages in the entire Nevi'im. The reference to the Book of the Upright in verse 13 — Sefer HaYashar — confirms that Yehoshua drew on earlier written sources now lost.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 14 — there has been no day like it before or since, when YHWH heeded the voice of a man, for YHWH fought for Yisra'EL — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's direct participation in the southern campaign. YHWH is the primary combatant.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 10. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The hailstones of verse 11 — more died from hailstones than from Yisra'EL's swords — is consistent with YHWH's direct intervention in covenant warfare as described in the War Scroll.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12-14 — sun stand still at Giv'on and moon in the Valley of Ayalon. The request is not for extra daylight — it is for the completion of the covenant judgment before nightfall. The sun standing still is YHWH's response to a man's voice in the midst of covenant warfare. Verse 14 — there has been no day like it before or since, when YHWH heeded the voice of a man. The uniqueness of the day is not the astronomical event alone — it is that YHWH responded to a human voice in this way. Covenant prayer at the level of commanding the created order. The text does not rationalize or explain the mechanism. It declares the theological fact: YHWH fought for Yisra'EL.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 11 The Northern Campaign — The Conquest Complete — Yehoshua Did as YHWH Commanded Moshe

1 When Yavin king of Khatzor heard of this, he sent to Yovav king of Madon, and to the king of Shimron, and to the king of Akhshaph,

2 And to the kings who were in the northern hill country, and in the Aravah south of Kinneroth, and in the lowland, and in Naphot-Dor on the west,

3 To the Kena'anites in the east and the west, the Amorites, the Khittites, the Perizzites, and the Yevusites in the hill country, and the Khivvites under Hermon in the land of Mitzpah.

4 And they came out with all their troops, a great horde, in number like the sand that is on the seashore, with very many horses and chariots.

5 And all these kings joined their forces and came and encamped together at the waters of Merom to fight against Yisra'EL.

6 And YHWH said to Yehoshua, Do not be afraid of them, for tomorrow at this time I will give over all of them, slain, to Yisra'EL. You shall hamstring their horses and burn their chariots with fire.

7 So Yehoshua and all his warriors came suddenly against them by the waters of Merom and fell upon them.

8 And YHWH gave them into the hand of Yisra'EL, who struck them and chased them as far as Great Tzidon and Misrephot-Maim, and eastward as far as the Valley of Mitzpeh. And they struck them until he left none remaining.

9 And Yehoshua did to them just as YHWH said to him — he hamstrung their horses and burned their chariots with fire.

10 And Yehoshua turned back at that time and captured Khatzor and struck its king with the sword, for Khatzor formerly was the head of all those kingdoms.

11 And they struck with the sword all who were in it, devoting them to destruction — there was none left that breathed. And he burned Khatzor with fire.

12 And Yehoshua captured all the cities of those kings, and all their kings, and struck them with the edge of the sword, devoting them to destruction, just as Moshe the servant of YHWH had commanded.

13 But none of the cities that stood on mounds did Yisra'EL burn, except Khatzor alone — that Yehoshua burned.

14 And all the spoil of these cities and the livestock, the people of Yisra'EL took for their plunder. But every person they struck with the edge of the sword until they had destroyed them, and they did not leave any who breathed.

15 Just as YHWH had commanded Moshe his servant, so Moshe commanded Yehoshua, and so Yehoshua did. He left nothing undone of all that YHWH had commanded Moshe.

16 So Yehoshua took all that land, the hill country and all the Negev and all the land of Goshen and the lowland and the Aravah and the hill country of Yisra'EL and its lowland

17 From Mount Khalak, which rises toward Se'ir, as far as Ba'al-Gad in the Valley of Lebanon below Mount Hermon. And he captured all their kings and struck them and put them to death.

18 Yehoshua made war a long time with all those kings.

19 There was not a city that made peace with the people of Yisra'EL except the Khivvites, the inhabitants of Giv'on. They took all in battle.

20 For it was YHWH's doing to harden their hearts that they should come against Yisra'EL in battle, in order that they should be devoted to destruction and should receive no mercy but be destroyed, just as YHWH commanded Moshe.

21 And Yehoshua came at that time and cut off the Anakim from the hill country, from Khevron, from Devir, from Anav, and from all the hill country of Yehudah, and from all the hill country of Yisra'EL. Yehoshua devoted them to destruction with their cities.

22 There was none of the Anakim left in the land of the people of Yisra'EL. Only in Azzah, in Gat, and in Ashdod did some remain.

23 So Yehoshua took the whole land, according to all that YHWH had spoken to Moshe. And Yehoshua gave it for an inheritance to Yisra'EL according to their tribal allotments. And the land had rest from war.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 11. Verse 15 is one of the most theologically significant verses in Yehoshua: just as YHWH commanded Moshe, so Moshe commanded Yehoshua, and so Yehoshua did — he left nothing undone of all that YHWH commanded Moshe. The chain of covenant transmission is complete.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 23 — so Yehoshua took the whole land, according to all that YHWH had spoken to Moshe — the land had rest from war — is read as the fulfillment of the covenant promise to Avraham in Bereshit 15:18-21.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 11. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The Anakim — the giants of verse 21-22 — are connected to the Nephilim tradition of Bereshit 6 and the spies' report in Bamidbar 13:33.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — he left nothing undone of all that YHWH had commanded Moshe. This is the covenant report card of Yehoshua's entire campaign: zero omissions. The contrast with Akhan's hidden transgression in Chapter 7 and the Giv'onite deception in Chapter 9 makes this declaration significant. Despite the failures within the covenant community, Yehoshua himself maintained complete obedience to the Mosaic commission. Verse 23 — the land had rest from war. The Hebrew shaqat — to be quiet, to rest — is the same word used for the covenant Sabbath rest. The land's rest from war is the covenant rest promised since Devarim 12:9-10.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 12 The Kings Defeated by Moshe — The Kings Defeated by Yehoshua — Thirty-One Kings in Total

1 Now these are the kings of the land whom the people of Yisra'EL defeated and took possession of their land beyond the Yarden toward the sunrise, from the Valley of Arnon to Mount Hermon, with all the Aravah to the east:

2 Sikhon king of the Amorites who lived at Kheshbon and ruled from Aro'er, which is on the edge of the Valley of Arnon, and from the middle of the valley as far as the river Yabbok, the boundary of the Ammonites, that is, half of Gil'ad,

3 And the Aravah to the Sea of Kinneroth eastward, and in the direction of Beit-Yeshimot, to the Sea of the Aravah, the Salt Sea, southward to the foot of the slopes of Pisgah.

4 And Og king of Bashan, one of the remnant of the Rephaim, who lived at Ashtarot and at Edre'i

5 And ruled over Mount Hermon and Salkhah and all Bashan to the boundary of the Geshurites and the Ma'akhathites, and over half of Gil'ad to the boundary of Sikhon king of Kheshbon.

6 Moshe, the servant of YHWH, and the people of Yisra'EL defeated them. And Moshe the servant of YHWH gave their land for a possession to the Reuvenites and the Gadites and the half-tribe of Menasheh.

7 And these are the kings of the land whom Yehoshua and the people of Yisra'EL defeated on the west side of the Yarden, from Ba'al-Gad in the Valley of Lebanon to Mount Khalak, that rises toward Se'ir — and Yehoshua gave their land to the tribes of Yisra'EL as a possession according to their allotments,

8 In the hill country, in the lowland, in the Aravah, in the slopes, in the wilderness, and in the Negev, the land of the Khittites, the Amorites, the Kena'anites, the Perizzites, the Khivvites, and the Yevusites:

9 The king of Yerikho, one — the king of Ai, which is beside Beit-El, one —

10 The king of Yerushalayim, one — the king of Khevron, one —

11 The king of Yarmut, one — the king of Lakhish, one —

12 The king of Eglon, one — the king of Gezer, one —

13 The king of Devir, one — the king of Geder, one —

14 The king of Khormah, one — the king of Arad, one —

15 The king of Livnah, one — the king of Adullam, one —

16 The king of Maqkedah, one — the king of Beit-El, one —

17 The king of Tappuakh, one — the king of Khefer, one —

18 The king of Afek, one — the king of Lashsharon, one —

19 The king of Madon, one — the king of Khatzor, one —

20 The king of Shimron-Meron, one — the king of Akhshaph, one —

21 The king of Ta'anakh, one — the king of Megiddo, one —

22 The king of Kedesh, one — the king of Yokne'am in Karmel, one —

23 The king of Dor in Naphot-Dor, one — the king of Goiim in Gilgal, one —

24 The king of Tirzah, one — in all, thirty-one kings.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Yehoshua. Chapter 12 is the covenant census of conquest — a formal enumeration of all kings defeated by both Moshe and Yehoshua. The list functions as the covenant deed of transfer: these lands, held by these kings, now belong to Yisra'EL by YHWH's decree.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. The list of thirty-one defeated kings is read as the historical confirmation of the covenant promise to Avraham — every nation and territory named in Bereshit 15:18-21 now accounted for.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 12. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. The formal catalog structure — the king of X, one — repeated twenty-four times in verses 9-24 is a covenant legal formula, not merely a narrative device.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The thirty-one kings of Chapter 12 are the covenant accounting of the conquest. Each king represents not merely a defeated enemy but a covenant territory claimed. The repetition — the king of X, one — functions as a legal covenant register: each entry closes a claim. Verse 6 names Moshe as the one who defeated Sikhon and Og — the TransJordan kings — and verse 7 names Yehoshua as the one who defeated the Cisjordan kings. The entire land west and east of the Yarden is now documented as covenant possession. The list is the deed.

THE ALLOTMENT OF THE LAND — CHAPTERS 13 THROUGH 15

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 13 Much Land Remains — The Allotment East of the Yarden — Levi Has No Portion — YHWH Is Their Inheritance

1 Now Yehoshua was old and advanced in years, and YHWH said to him, You are old and advanced in years, and there remains yet very much land to possess.

2 This is the land that yet remains: all the regions of the Pelishtim, and all those of the Geshurites

3 From the Shihor, which is east of Egypt, northward to the boundary of Ekron, it is counted as Kena'anite — the five lords of the Pelishtim, those of Azzah, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gat, and Ekron, and those of the Avvim,

4 In the south, all the land of the Kena'anites, and Me'arah that belongs to the Tzidonians, to Afek, to the boundary of the Amorites,

5 And the land of the Giblites, and all Lebanon, toward the sunrise, from Ba'al-Gad below Mount Hermon to Lebo-Khamath,

6 All the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon to Misrephot-Maim, even all the Tzidonians. I myself will drive them out from before the people of Yisra'EL. Only allot the land to Yisra'EL for an inheritance, as I have commanded you.

7 Now therefore divide this land for an inheritance to the nine tribes and half the tribe of Menasheh.

8 With the other half of Menasheh the Reuvenites and the Gadites received their inheritance, which Moshe gave them, beyond the Yarden eastward, as Moshe the servant of YHWH gave them:

9 From Aro'er, which is on the edge of the Valley of Arnon, and the city that is in the middle of the valley, and all the tableland of Medeva as far as Divon,

10 And all the cities of Sikhon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Kheshbon, as far as the boundary of the Ammonites,

11 And Gil'ad, and the region of the Geshurites and Ma'akhathites, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan to Salkhah,

12 All the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtarot and in Edre'i — he alone was left of the remnant of the Rephaim — these Moshe had defeated and driven out.

13 Yet the people of Yisra'EL did not drive out the Geshurites or the Ma'akhathites, but Geshur and Ma'akhat dwell in the midst of Yisra'EL to this day.

14 To the tribe of Levi alone Moshe gave no inheritance. The offerings by fire to YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL are their inheritance, as he said to them.

15 And Moshe gave an inheritance to the tribe of the people of Reuven according to their clans.

16 So their territory was from Aro'er, which is on the edge of the Valley of Arnon, and the city that is in the middle of the valley, and all the tableland by Medeva,

17 With Kheshbon, and all its cities that are in the tableland — Divon, Bamot-Ba'al, Beit-Ba'al-Me'on,

18 Yahatz, Kedemot, Mefa'at,

19 Kiryatayim, Sivmah, Tzeret-Shakhar on the hill of the valley,

20 Beit-Pe'or, the slopes of Pisgah, Beit-HaYeshimot,

21 All the cities of the tableland, and all the kingdom of Sikhon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Kheshbon, whom Moshe defeated with the leaders of Midyan, Evi, Rekem, Tzur, Khur, and Reva, the princes of Sikhon, who lived in the land.

22 Bil'am also, the son of Be'or, the one who practiced divination, was killed with the sword by the people of Yisra'EL among the rest of their slain.

23 And the border of the people of Reuven was the Yarden as their western boundary. This was the inheritance of the people of Reuven, according to their clans with their cities and villages.

24 And Moshe gave an inheritance also to the tribe of Gad, to the people of Gad, according to their clans.

25 Their territory was Ya'zer, and all the cities of Gil'ad, and half the land of the Ammonites, to Aro'er, which is east of Rabbah,

26 And from Kheshbon to Ramat-Mitzpeh and Betonim, and from Makhanayim to the territory of Devir,

27 And in the valley Beit-Haram, Beit-Nimrah, Sukkot, and Tzafon, the rest of the kingdom of Sikhon king of Kheshbon, with the Yarden as the boundary, to the lower end of the Sea of Kinneroth, beyond the Yarden eastward.

28 This is the inheritance of the people of Gad according to their clans, with their cities and villages.

29 And Moshe gave an inheritance to the half-tribe of Menasheh. It was allotted to the half-tribe of the people of Menasheh according to their clans.

30 Their region extended from Makhanayim, through all of Bashan, the whole kingdom of Og king of Bashan, and all the towns of Ya'ir, which are in Bashan, sixty cities,

31 And half of Gil'ad, and Ashtarot, and Edre'i, the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan. These were allotted to the people of Makhir the son of Menasheh for the half of the people of Makhir according to their clans.

32 These are the inheritances that Moshe distributed in the plains of Moav, beyond the Yarden east of Yerikho.

33 But to the tribe of Levi, Moshe gave no inheritance. YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL is their inheritance, as he said to them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 13. The chapter opens with YHWH's declaration that Yehoshua is old and much land remains — verse 1 — establishing that the covenant inheritance is not yet fully realized even after the military campaigns. The allotment begins despite the incompleteness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 14 and 33 — to the tribe of Levi Moshe gave no inheritance — YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL is their inheritance — is read as the covenant definition of the Levitical calling: the service of YHWH is itself the possession.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 13. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 22 — Bil'am the son of Be'or, the one who practiced divination, was killed with the sword — is the brief but significant notice of the fate of the prophet-for-hire of Bamidbar 22-24.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 and 33 — YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL is their inheritance, as he said to them. The Levitical tribe receives no land portion. YHWH himself is their inheritance. This is the most profound statement of covenant vocation in the entire allotment section: the Levites' inheritance is not territory but YHWH. The same principle appears in Psalm 16:5 — YHWH is my chosen portion and my cup — and in Psalm 73:26 — YHWH is my portion forever. The Levitical inheritance becomes the aspiration of every covenant person: to have YHWH himself as the substance of life.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 14 Kalev Requests His Inheritance — Forty-Five Years I Have Followed YHWH Fully — Khevron Given to Kalev

1 These are the inheritances that the people of Yisra'EL received in the land of Kena'an, which Ele'azar the priest and Yehoshua the son of Nun and the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of the people of Yisra'EL gave them to inherit.

2 Their inheritance was by lot, just as YHWH had commanded by the hand of Moshe for the nine and one-half tribes.

3 For Moshe had given an inheritance to the two and one-half tribes beyond the Yarden, but to the Levites he gave no inheritance among them.

4 For the people of Yosef were two tribes, Menasheh and Efrayim. And no portion was given to the Levites in the land, but only cities to dwell in, with their pasturelands for their livestock and their substance.

5 The people of Yisra'EL did as YHWH commanded Moshe — they allotted the land.

6 Then the people of Yehudah came to Yehoshua at Gilgal. And Kalev the son of Yefunneh the Kenizzite said to him, You know what YHWH said to Moshe the man of Creator in Kadesh-Barnea concerning you and me.

7 I was forty years old when Moshe the servant of YHWH sent me from Kadesh-Barnea to spy out the land, and I brought him word again as it was in my heart.

8 But my brothers who went up with me made the heart of the people melt — yet I wholly followed YHWH my Creator.

9 And Moshe swore on that day, saying, Surely the land on which your foot has trodden shall be an inheritance for you and your children forever, because you have wholly followed YHWH my Creator.

10 And now, behold, YHWH has kept me alive, just as he said, these forty-five years since the time that YHWH spoke this word to Moshe, while Yisra'EL walked in the wilderness. And now, behold, I am this day eighty-five years old.

11 I am still as strong today as I was in the day that Moshe sent me — my strength now is as my strength was then, for war and for going and coming.

12 So now give me this hill country of which YHWH spoke on that day, for you heard on that day how the Anakim were there, with great fortified cities. It may be that YHWH will be with me, and I shall drive them out just as YHWH said.

13 Then Yehoshua blessed him, and he gave Khevron to Kalev the son of Yefunneh for an inheritance.

14 Therefore Khevron became the inheritance of Kalev the son of Yefunneh the Kenizzite to this day, because he wholly followed YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

15 Now the name of Khevron formerly was Kiryat-Arba. Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim. And the land had rest from war.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 14. Kalev's request at age eighty-five is one of the most remarkable individual covenant narratives in Yehoshua — forty-five years after Moshe's promise, Kalev claims exactly what was promised, standing as strong as the day he was sent.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Kalev's declaration — I wholly followed YHWH my Creator — verses 8 and 14 — is read in the Beta Israel tradition as the covenant standard of complete, undivided allegiance to YHWH sustained across a lifetime.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 14. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Kalev the Kenizzite is a non-Israelite by birth — verse 6 — yet his covenant faithfulness surpassed the native-born Israelites who refused to enter the land. His inheritance at Khevron — the burial place of Avraham — is the covenant reward of a lifetime of trust.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10-12 — YHWH has kept me alive these forty-five years — I am this day eighty-five years old — I am still as strong as I was — give me this hill country. Kalev does not ask for an easy portion in his old age. He asks for the hardest territory — the Anakim's fortified hill country. His reasoning is covenant logic: it may be that YHWH will be with me and I shall drive them out just as YHWH said. At eighty-five, Kalev still operates on the same covenant confidence that distinguished him at forty. The forty-five year wait did not produce bitterness or reduced expectation — it produced the same faith that had separated him from the ten faithless spies in Bamidbar 13.

YEHOSHUA — CHAPTER 15 The Allotment of Yehudah — Kalev Takes Khevron — Otni'el Son of Kenaz — Cities of Yehudah

1 The allotment for the tribe of the people of Yehudah according to their clans reached southward to the boundary of Edom, to the wilderness of Tzin at the farthest south.

2 And their south boundary ran from the end of the Salt Sea, from the bay that faces southward.

3 It goes out southward of the ascent of Akrabbim, passes along to Tzin, and goes up south of Kadesh-Barnea, along by Khetzron, up to Addar, turns about to Karka,

4 Passes along to Atzmon, goes out by the Brook of Egypt, and the boundary comes to an end at the sea. This shall be your south boundary.

5 And the east boundary is the Salt Sea, to the mouth of the Yarden. And the boundary on the north side runs from the bay of the sea at the mouth of the Yarden.

6 And the boundary goes up to Beit-Khoglah and passes along north of Beit-Aravah. And the boundary goes up to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuven.

7 And the boundary goes up to Devir from the Valley of Akhor, and so northward, turning toward Gilgal, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the valley. And the boundary passes along to the waters of En-Shemesh and ends at En-Rogel.

8 Then the boundary goes up by the Valley of the Son of Hinnom at the southern shoulder of the Yevusite — that is, Yerushalayim. And the boundary goes up to the top of the mountain that lies before the Valley of Hinnom, on the west, at the northern end of the Valley of Refa'im.

9 Then the boundary extends from the top of the mountain to the spring of the waters of Neftokha, and from there to the cities of Mount Efron. Then the boundary bends to Ba'alah — that is, Kiryat-Ye'arim.

10 And the boundary circles west of Ba'alah to Mount Se'ir, passes along to the northern shoulder of Mount Ye'arim — that is, Kesalon — and goes down to Beit-Shemesh and passes along by Timnah.

11 The boundary goes out to the shoulder of Ekron to the north, then the boundary bends to Shikkeron and passes along to Mount Ba'alah and goes out to Yavne'el. Then the boundary comes to an end at the sea.

12 And the west boundary was the Great Sea with its coastline. This is the boundary around the people of Yehudah according to their clans.

13 According to the commandment of YHWH to Yehoshua, he gave to Kalev the son of Yefunneh a portion among the people of Yehudah, Kiryat-Arba, the city of Arba the father of Anak — that is, Khevron.

14 And Kalev drove out from there the three sons of Anak, Sheshai and Akhiman and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.

15 And he went up from there against the inhabitants of Devir. Now the name of Devir formerly was Kiryat-Sefer.

16 And Kalev said, Whoever strikes Kiryat-Sefer and captures it, to him will I give Akhsah my daughter as wife.

17 And Otni'el the son of Kenaz, the brother of Kalev, captured it. And he gave him Akhsah his daughter as wife.

18 When she came to him, she urged him to ask her father for a field. And she got off her donkey, and Kalev said to her, What do you want?

19 She said to him, Give me a blessing. Since you have given me the land of the Negev, give me also springs of water. And he gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

20 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Yehudah according to their clans.

21 The cities belonging to the tribe of the people of Yehudah in the extreme south, toward the boundary of Edom, were Kavtze'el, Eder, Yagur,

22 Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,

23 Kedesh, Khattzor, Itnan,

24 Ziph, Telem, Be'alot,

25 Khattzor-Khadattah, Keriot-Khetzron — that is, Khattzor —

26 Amam, Shema, Moladah,

27 Khattzar-Gaddah, Kheshmon, Beit-Pelet,

28 Khattzar-Shu'al, Be'er-Sheva, Bizyoteyah,

29 Ba'alah, Iyim, Etzem,

30 Eltolad, Kesil, Khormah,

31 Tziklag, Madmannah, Sansannah,

32 Levavot, Shilkhim, Ayin, and Rimmon — in all, twenty-nine cities with their villages.

33 And in the lowland, Eshtaol, Tzorah, Ashnah,

34 Zanoakh, En-Gannim, Tappuakh, Enam,

35 Yarmut, Adullam, Sokhoh, Azekah,

36 Sha'arayim, Aditayim, Gederah, Gederotayim — fourteen cities with their villages.

37 Tzenan, Khadashah, Migdal-Gad,

38 Dil'an, Mitzpeh, Yokthe'el,

39 Lakhish, Botzkat, Eglon,

40 Kabbon, Lakhmam, Kitlish,

41 Gederot, Beit-Dagon, Na'amah, and Maqkedah — sixteen cities with their villages.

42 Livnah, Eter, Ashan,

43 Yiftakh, Ashnah, Netziv,

44 Ke'ilah, Akhziv, and Mareshah — nine cities with their villages.

45 Ekron, with its towns and its villages.

46 From Ekron to the sea, all that were by the side of Ashdod, with their villages.

47 Ashdod, its towns and its villages — Azzah, its towns and its villages — to the Brook of Egypt, and the Great Sea with its coastline.

48 And in the hill country, Shamir, Yattir, Sokhoh,

49 Dannah, Kiryat-Sannah — that is, Devir —

50 Anav, Eshtemoh, Anim,

51 Goshen, Kholon, and Giloh — eleven cities with their villages.

52 Arab, Dumah, Eshan,

53 Yanum, Beit-Tappuakh, Afekah,

54 Khumtah, Kiryat-Arba — that is, Khevron — and Tziyor — nine cities with their villages.

55 Ma'on, Karmel, Ziph, Yuttah,

56 Yizre'el, Yokde'am, Zanoakh,

57 Kayin, Giv'ah, Timnah — ten cities with their villages.

58 Khal-Kul, Beit-Tzur, Gedor,

59 Ma'arat, Beit-Anot, and Eltekon — six cities with their villages.

60 Kiryat-Ba'al — that is, Kiryat-Ye'arim — and Rabbah — two cities with their villages.

61 In the wilderness, Beit-Aravah, Middin, Sekhakhah,

62 Nivshan, the City of Salt, and En-Gedi — six cities with their villages.

63 But the Yevusites, the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, the people of Yehudah could not drive out, so the Yevusites dwell with the people of Yehudah at Yerushalayim to this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJosha (4Q47) preserves fragmentary portions of Chapter 15. The extensive city lists of verses 21-62 are covenant cadastral records — the formal territorial inventory of Yehudah's inheritance. The DSS community's own meticulous record-keeping of the New Jerusalem in the Temple Scroll reflects the same covenant concern for precise territorial documentation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. The account of Akhsah — verses 16-19 — who asks her father Kalev for both the land of the Negev and the springs of water is read as the covenant model of covenant inheritance: she asked for the complete provision, not merely the land but the water that makes it life-giving.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in Yehoshua Chapter 15. The Masoretic text aligns with the DSS reading. Verse 63 — the Yevusites dwell with the people of Yehudah at Yerushalayim to this day — is the honest covenant admission that the complete inheritance was not fully realized in Yehoshua's generation. Yerushalayim was not taken until Dawid in 2 Shemu'el 5.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19 — give me a blessing. Since you have given me the land of the Negev, give me also springs of water. Akhsah's request is the covenant logic of inheritance extended: the land gift is incomplete without the water that sustains it. Kalev gives both the upper and lower springs. The covenant inheritance is not merely territory but the full provision of life within the territory. Verse 63 — the Yevusites the people of Yehudah could not drive out — they dwell at Yerushalayim to this day. The city that will become the covenant capital of Yisra'EL is not yet taken at the end of Yehoshua. YHWH's covenant promise is fulfilled progressively, not instantaneously. The full realization awaits Dawid.

END OF PART TWO — CHAPTERS 9 THROUGH 15
